uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame; expected.data produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (ORACLE) on this input and re-keying to the normalized vocabulary (Humidity->air.relativeHumidity, Temperature->air.temperature; RPM/FPM/EAQI/Version have no vocabulary key -> camelCase extras).
Synthetic in-bounds frame exercising the upstream signed-temperature path (bytes[2]<<24>>16|bytes[3] => -55 => -5.5 C). expected.data from the ORACLE, re-keyed.

uplink.description: fw 1.5 (fPort 2): adds pressure, CO/NH3/NO2 gases and noise
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame (pressure 1013 hPa within the vocabulary 900..1100 bound). expected.data from the ORACLE, re-keyed: Pressure->air.pressure; CO/NH3/NO2/Noise/Version have no vocabulary key -> camelCase extras (the Pengy reports CO/NH3/NO2, not CO2, so no air.co2).

uplink.description: fw 2.0 (fPort 3): temperature + humidity + pressure + PM1/PM2.5/PM10 + noise
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame (pressure 998 hPa within 900..1100). expected.data from the ORACLE, re-keyed: UPM/FPM/RPM (PM1/PM2.5/PM10) -> pm1/pm25/pm10 extras; EAQI/Noise/Version -> camelCase extras.
Synthetic error case: upstream silently returns an empty data object for any fPort other than 1/2/3; this codec instead reports an error so the caller is not handed a bare {}.
